seoTitle: How to Enter a Partial Payment in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to enter a partial payment in QuickBooks Online for bills or invoices, keeping the remaining balance accurate until it's fully paid.
intro: Whether you're paying a bill or receiving money from a customer, sometimes only part of the amount changes hands at once. This guide explains how to enter a partial payment in QuickBooks Online so the remaining balance stays correct.

steps.heading: Open the bill or invoice
steps.body: Navigate to the specific transaction that's only being partially paid.

steps.heading: Choose the payment option
steps.body: Select pay bill or receive payment depending on whether you're paying a vendor or getting paid by a customer.

steps.heading: Enter the partial amount
steps.body: Type in the actual amount being paid or received instead of the full balance.

steps.heading: Save and check the remaining balance
steps.body: Confirm the transaction saved correctly and that the outstanding balance reflects what's still owed.

faq.question: Can I make more than one partial payment on the same bill?
faq.answer: Yes, you can enter multiple partial payments until the full balance is paid off.

faq.question: Does a partial payment change the due date?
faq.answer: No, the original due date stays the same; only the remaining balance is affected.

faq.question: How do I know how much is still owed?
faq.answer: The bill or invoice will show the updated outstanding balance after each partial payment is recorded.

faq.question: What if I overpay by mistake?
faq.answer: An overpayment will typically show as a credit that you can apply to future bills or invoices.
